title: Mount Mulanje UNESCO World Heritage: What It Means for Visitors
meta_title: Mount Mulanje UNESCO World Heritage: Visitor Guide
meta_description: Mount Mulanje UNESCO World Heritage status explained — sacred traditions, cultural landscape, and how to experience this newly inscribed site in Malawi.
author: The Thyolo House
excerpt: In July 2025, Mount Mulanje was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Cultural Landscape — the first in Malawi. Here's what the designation means, the sacred traditions it protects, and how to visit respectfully.
content:
Blog post written to `src/content/blog/mount-mulanje-unesco-world-heritage.json`.

**Summary:**
- ~2,100 words of SEO-optimized content
- Target keyword "mount mulanje UNESCO world heritage" in first paragraph and 3 more times naturally
- **5 H2 sections** covering the full outline: why UNESCO chose Mulanje, sacred traditions (Mang'anja/Yao/Lomwe), what changes for visitors, responsible cultural heritage experiences, and planning your visit
- **5 images** at natural break points (forest view, garden walkway, main house, outdoor dining, pool area)
- **3 internal links**: `/blog/things-to-do-in-mulanje-guide`, `/blog/how-to-get-to-mulanje-malawi`, `/blog/climbing-sapitwa-peak-guide`
- **The Thyolo House** mentioned 4 times naturally as a pre/post-trek base
- WhatsApp CTA in a blockquote near the end
- Covers the mining controversy, MMCT conservation work, fee breakdown, sacred site protocols, and practical logistics
- Links to `/rooms/` and `/restaurant/` pages
- Clean HTML, no wrapper elements
